__schema0.title: Reference Object
__schema0.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

examples.description: A user is a type of person.
A pet is a type of animal.
A pet is a type of animal.

__schema1.title: Reference
__schema1.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

__schema5.title: summary
__schema5.description: A short summary which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema6.title: description
__schema6.description: A description which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema9.title: name
__schema9.description: The name of the query parameter. Case-sensitive.

__schema11.title: description
__schema11.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema16.description: Allows empty values (?param=). Deprecated in favor of schema-level validation.
__schema16.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema19.title: explode
__schema19.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema20.description: When true, allows reserved characters without percent-encoding.
__schema20.title: allowReserved

__schema23.title: Schema Object
__schema23.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema24.title: String Object
__schema24.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema25.title: title
__schema25.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema26.title: description
__schema26.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema27.title: default
__schema27.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema28.title: example
__schema28.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema31.title: enum
__schema31.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema33.title: const
__schema33.description: A constant value that this schema must match.

__schema35.title: Discriminator
__schema35.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema36.title: propertyName
__schema36.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema37.title: mapping
__schema37.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema40.title: defaultMapping
__schema40.description: The default mapping to use when no matching discriminator value is found.

__schema43.title: XML
__schema43.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema44.title: name
__schema44.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema45.title: namespace
__schema45.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema46.title: prefix
__schema46.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema47.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema47.title: attribute

__schema49.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema49.title: wrapped

__schema53.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema53.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema54.title: description
__schema54.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema55.title: url
__schema55.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema57.title: readOnly
__schema57.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema58.title: writeOnly
__schema58.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema59.title: deprecated
__schema59.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema61.title: format
__schema61.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema62.title: Pattern
__schema62.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema69.title: contentMediaType
__schema69.description: Media type of the encoded string content.

__schema72.title: Number Object
__schema72.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema74.title: format
__schema74.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema75.title: multipleOf
__schema75.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema77.title: minimum
__schema77.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema79.title: maximum
__schema79.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema81.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema81.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema83.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema83.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema85.title: Integer Object
__schema85.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema87.title: format
__schema87.description: Integer size format. Use 'int32' for signed 32-bit (-2B to 2B) or 'int64' for signed 64-bit integers.

__schema88.title: multipleOf
__schema88.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema90.title: minimum
__schema90.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema92.title: maximum
__schema92.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema94.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema94.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema96.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema96.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema98.title: Boolean Object
__schema98.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema100.title: Null Object
__schema100.description: A Null Object defines the shape of a null value.

__schema102.title: Array Object
__schema102.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema106.title: prefixItems
__schema106.description: Tuple validation: schemas for items by index (array position).

__schema108.title: contains
__schema108.description: Require the array to contain at least one item matching this subschema.

__schema120.title: Object Object
__schema120.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema144.title: Composition Schema
__schema144.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not, if/then/else) without a type constraint.

__schema145.title: allOf
__schema145.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema147.title: anyOf
__schema147.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema149.title: oneOf
__schema149.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema151.title: not
__schema151.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema153.title: if
__schema153.description: Conditional validation: if the instance matches this schema, apply then; otherwise apply else.

__schema157.title: else
__schema157.description: Subschema applied when if does not match.

__schema160.title: examples
__schema160.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema167.title: externalValue
__schema167.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema168.title: dataValue
__schema168.description: The data value of the example before serialization. Mutually exclusive with value and externalValue.

__schema169.title: serializedValue
__schema169.description: The serialized representation of the example. Mutually exclusive with value and externalValue.

__schema171.title: content
__schema171.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema173.title: MediaType
__schema173.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema181.title: Encoding
__schema181.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema182.title: contentType
__schema182.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema185.title: Header
__schema185.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema186.title: Header
__schema186.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema187.title: description
__schema187.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema188.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema188.title: required

__schema190.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema190.title: deprecated

__schema192.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema192.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema194.title: style
__schema194.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.

__schema218.title: itemSchema
__schema218.description: Schema for individual items in streaming responses.

__schema219.title: itemEncoding
__schema219.description: Encoding for individual items in streaming responses.

__schema224.title: name
__schema224.description: The name of the path parameter. Must match a template expression in the path.

__schema226.title: description
__schema226.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema227.description: Path parameters must always be required.
__schema227.title: required

__schema231.title: explode
__schema231.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema234.title: examples
__schema234.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema236.title: content
__schema236.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema240.title: name
__schema240.description: The name of the header. Case-insensitive.

__schema242.title: description
__schema242.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema247.title: style
__schema247.description: Serialization style. Only 'simple' is valid for headers.

__schema248.title: explode
__schema248.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema251.title: examples
__schema251.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema253.title: content
__schema253.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema259.title: description
__schema259.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema264.title: style
__schema264.description: Serialization style. Only 'form' is valid for cookies.

__schema265.title: explode
__schema265.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema268.title: examples
__schema268.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema270.title: content
__schema270.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.
